reasoning: The question asks whether CMI's closing price will be above its 2026-02-01 baseline (which is the 2026-01-30 close of $576.88) on two future dates: 2026-02-08 (resolving to Feb 6) and 2026-03-03. Cummins is set to report its Q4 2025 earnings on February 5, 2026, before the market opens. Entering this event, the stock has experienced a notable rally from around $417 in late October to ~$577 today. Recent mid-January analyst upgrades (e.g., Citi to $630, Truist to $653) signal strong bullish sentiment regarding their 2026 guidance reinstatement, which may offset previously telegraphed Q4 weakness (a 15% drop in NA on-highway shipments). The underlying linear trend estimates the short-term probability of being above the threshold at ~0.54. Because earnings reactions inherently add pure variance, the forecasted probability of an upward move is slightly muted but remains moderately positive due to the general market drift and bullish target prices. Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon, the probability for the distant resolution date is adjusted to be closer to 0.50.
